schema_classification.note: Partially covered files use agent-discovered span names (mcp.*) but some attributes (journal.file_path) match the base registry.

NDS-001.evidence: All 9 committed files pass node --check validation (orbweaver pipeline validated before commit).

NDS-002.evidence: Test suite passes for committed files (orbweaver pipeline runs tests before commit). Failed/partial files were NOT committed, preserving test suite integrity.

API-001.evidence: All 9 committed files import only from '@opentelemetry/api'. No SDK or instrumentation package imports in source files.

NDS-006.evidence: All files use ESM imports. No require() or module.exports detected. Project has 'type': 'module' in package.json.

API-002.evidence: @opentelemetry/api is in peerDependencies (correct for library project). Agent also added @traceloop/instrumentation-langchain and @traceloop/instrumentation-mcp as optional peerDependencies — these are auto-instrumentation packages, not vendor-specific SDKs, but adding them to a library's dependencies is architecturally questionable (see note).
API-002.note: Library projects should depend only on the OTel API. The @traceloop packages are auto-instrumentation libraries that belong in the deployer's SDK configuration, not the library's package.json. However, they are marked as optional peerDependencies, which softens the impact.

API-003.evidence: No vendor-specific SDK packages (dd-trace, @newrelic/telemetry-sdk, @splunk/otel, etc.) found in package.json.

API-004.evidence: No imports from @opentelemetry/sdk-*, @opentelemetry/exporter-*, or @opentelemetry/instrumentation-* in any source file.

CDQ-008.evidence: All 9 committed files use trace.getTracer('commit-story'). Single naming pattern — project name from package.json. No outliers.

NDS-003.evidence: Only instrumentation additions: import, tracer, span wrapping, attributes, try/catch/finally. Business logic preserved.

NDS-004.evidence: collectChatMessages signature preserved. 7 other exported functions unmodified.

NDS-005.evidence: No pre-existing error handling modified. Agent added new try/catch/finally for span lifecycle only.

COV-001.evidence: collectChatMessages (exported async entry point) has span.

COV-002.evidence: No outbound network/DB calls. Internal fs operations via sync helpers.

COV-003.evidence: Span has try/catch/finally with recordException + setStatus(ERROR) + re-throw.

COV-005.evidence: 5 attributes set, all matching registry: context.source, context.time_window_start/end, context.sessions_count, context.messages_count.

COV-006.evidence: No auto-instrumentation library covers file system operations.

RST-001.evidence: Only the main exported async function instrumented. No utility functions have spans.

RST-004.evidence: 7 unexported helper functions (getClaudeProjectsDir, findJSONLFiles, etc.) not instrumented.

SCH-002.evidence: All 5 attribute keys match registry definitions (context.source, time_window_start/end, sessions_count, messages_count).

SCH-003.evidence: context.source='claude_code' matches enum. Counts are ints. Timestamps are strings (ISO 8601).

CDQ-003.evidence: Standard pattern: recordException(error) + setStatus({code: ERROR}) + re-throw.

CDQ-006.evidence: toISOString() calls exempt (cheap computation). sessions.size and allMessages.length are property access.

CDQ-007.evidence: All attributes are bounded (counts, timestamps, enum). No PII.

NDS-003.evidence: Only instrumentation additions. Existing error handling in runGit preserved inside span callback.

NDS-004.evidence: getPreviousCommitTime and getCommitData signatures preserved. runGit (unexported) signature preserved.

NDS-005.evidence: runGit had existing try/catch with error.code === 128 checks. Agent added recordException BEFORE existing handling. Re-throws are genuine errors (git failures), not expected conditions.

COV-001.evidence: getPreviousCommitTime and getCommitData (exported async entry points) have spans.

COV-002.evidence: runGit wraps execFileAsync('git', args) — subprocess execution — in a span.

COV-003.evidence: All 3 spans have error recording in catch blocks.

COV-005.evidence: Uses vcs.ref.head.revision (OTel semconv), commit_story.context.source, commit_story.commit.author/message/timestamp. All in registry.

RST-004.evidence: runGit is unexported but performs subprocess I/O (execFileAsync). RST-004 I/O exemption applies.

NDS-004.evidence: getChangedFiles, isMergeCommit, getCommitMetadata signatures preserved. isSafeGitRef, isJournalEntriesOnlyCommit, shouldSkipMergeCommit unmodified.

NDS-005.evidence: getChangedFiles and isMergeCommit have catch blocks returning defaults ([] and {isMerge: false}). Agent adds recordException + setStatus(ERROR). These are defensive fallbacks for git failures — genuinely errors, not expected conditions. Borderline NDS-005b but classified as pass: git failing IS an error, the function just handles it gracefully.

partial.quality_notes: Inconsistent span naming: generateMonthlySummary uses commit_story.ai.generate_monthly_summary while generateDailySummary uses commit_story.journal.generate_daily_summary (ai vs journal namespace). Would fail SCH-001 consistency if committed.

partial.nds_evaluation: No diff available for evaluation (partial diff not captured).

NDS-003.evidence: 1) Duplicate JSDoc blocks (DEEP-4). 2) const tracer placed between import statements (bad style, valid syntax). 3) Bare catch renamed to catch (e) without re-throwing change is acceptable.

NDS-005.evidence: saveJournalEntry: file-not-found catch (readFile for duplicate check) gets recordException + setStatus(ERROR). Original catch comment says 'File doesn't exist yet, proceed' — classic expected-condition handler. Recording this as an error pollutes metrics with normal first-run behavior.

NDS-003.evidence: 1) Duplicate JSDoc blocks on 7+ functions. 2) const tracer between import statements. Both are non-instrumentation changes.

NDS-005.evidence: 5 NDS-005b violations confirmed across 3 functions: (1) generateAndSaveDailySummary access() catch for 'summary exists' check, (2) generateAndSaveWeeklySummary same pattern, (3) saveMonthlySummary same pattern, (4) readMonthWeeklySummaries readdir catch for 'directory not found', (5) readMonthWeeklySummaries readFile catch for 'file unreadable'. All are expected-condition catches (file/directory not found = normal first-run) with added recordException + setStatus(ERROR). Independently confirmed by CodeRabbit CLI review.

getDaysWithEntries: 2 NDS-005b violations — (1) readdir(entriesDir) catch returns [] for 'entries directory not found' (expected on first run), (2) readdir(monthDir) catch continues loop for 'month directory unreadable'. Both are expected-condition handlers with added recordException + setStatus(ERROR).
